stem: In triangle ABC, AB = AC. An exterior angle at vertex A has measure 130°. What is the measure, in degrees, of angle B?
rationale: An exterior angle and its corresponding interior angle are supplementary, so ∠A = 180 − 130 = 50°. Since AB = AC, triangle ABC is isosceles with ∠B = ∠C. The interior angles sum to 180°, so ∠B = ∠C = (180 − 50)/2 = 65°.
